docs.title: The Agentic Operations Platform for Actimize
docs.text:
Sign in once, connect your Actimize products, and run your financial-crime operations by talking to an AI — grounded, safe, and auditable.

Actimize is best-in-class at financial-crime and compliance — but its value is gated behind scarce, certified specialists spread across many separate products and consoles. ActWise turns the whole estate into an AI-operable surface: one guarded backend that a client drives by voice or chat, from their own AI or a NiCE-branded front end.

Not a copilot — an operations layer

Every AML/fraud vendor is shipping the same feature: a text copilot that summarizes and drafts. ActWise is a different category — the AI layer that operates the case end-to-end (safely, cited, audited), not one that talks about it. That's the competitive moat.

The hard engineering is already built and proven against a live ActOne instance. This platform is about packaging those connectors into a sellable, tiered offering.

The 10X

Turn every analyst, implementer, and support engineer into an Actimize power user — and automate the repetitive work — over one backend that is read-only by default, gates every write, and audits everything.

docs.title: The three planes
docs.text:
• :material-book-open-variant: Knowledge plane — DOCenter

    Grounded, cited answers over the entire Actimize documentation corpus. Never invents a feature that doesn't exist; says "I don't know" when retrieval is empty.

  • :material-robot-industrial: Action plane — ActOne Ops

    Discovers the live REST/SOAP surface of a connected instance, reads work items / alerts / config / diagnostics, and performs gated actions by voice or chat over the open MCP standard.

  • :material-school: Learning plane — Academy

    Turns the same knowledge into role-based training, onboarding, and certification prep — the platform doesn't just operate Actimize, it teaches.

docs.title: One backend, five product tiers
docs.text:
The platform ships as one guarded backend with a packaging model (how it's distributed) and a hero product (what it does), plus expansion tiers that compound scope on the same backend.

Tier Role What it delivers A · Connector Suite Packaging All MCPs/connectors as an installable "Actimize AI Pack" — point your own AI at it. B · Agentic Workflow ⭐ Hero Voice-first, human-in-the-loop investigation engine with proactive batching. C · Control Tower Visibility Cross-product health dashboard + NL "render me a dashboard" + cited reports. D · Implementation Accelerator Services Download → stand-up → config-audit → cited upgrade/gap report. E · Academy Enablement Role-based training + cert prep + sandbox from the doc corpus.

docs.title: Start here
docs.text:
• :material-lightbulb-on: The opportunity — why the operating layer is the bottleneck.
  • :material-layers: The platform — how the tiers fit on one backend.
  • :material-shield-check: Safety & trust — the regulated-AI model.
  • :material-sword-cross: Competitive moat — why this displaces the field, not just competes.
  • :material-presentation-play: Demo storyboard — the sparkathon-style demo spine.

Internal product-vision document

This site is a 10X Idea product concept built on the ActWise toolkit. It is a forward-looking vision for discussion, not a shipped product or an official NiCE commitment. Revenue and impact figures are illustrative — validate with Product/Finance before external use.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The differentiated engineering — grounded knowledge retrieval and discovery-driven, gated live operations — already exists in the ActWise repo. Productizing the agentic/voice layer means assembling mature, permissively-licensed building blocks rather than inventing an orchestrator.

docs.title: Layered reference architecture
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    subgraph V["1 · Voice / chat layer"]
        VA["Azure OpenAI Realtime (gpt-realtime) · Entra ID<br/><small>or self-hosted: LiveKit + Deepgram STT + ElevenLabs TTS</small>"]
    end
    subgraph O["2 · Orchestration / multi-agent"]
        OG["Durable, human-in-the-loop graph<br/><small>LangGraph or Microsoft Agent Framework · MCP-native</small>"]
    end
    subgraph T["3 · Tool / integration layer — the ActWise connectors"]
        K["docenter"] --- AC["actone ops"] --- DT["actone-data"] --- UT["actone-utils"] --- SP["ndc / installer"]
    end
    subgraph G["4 · Human-in-the-loop write gate"]
        GA["Every mutating call pauses for explicit approval"]
    end
    subgraph AU["5 · Audit & compliance"]
        AL["OpenTelemetry traces → immutable log / SIEM"]
    end
    V --> O --> T
    T --> G --> AU
  1. Voice / chat layer. Microsoft path: Azure OpenAI Realtime over WebRTC with Entra ID, VNET, Azure Monitor — the production path for Teams/M365. Self-hosted / data-sovereign path: LiveKit Agents (Apache 2.0) + Deepgram STT + ElevenLabs TTS. A STT→LLM→TTS pipeline is preferred in a regulated context because every transcript and step is loggable.
  2. Orchestration. A durable, human-in-the-loop graph — LangGraph (MIT) or the Microsoft Agent Framework (MIT) when going Copilot-Studio-first. Investigation modeled as: Triage → Enrich → Hypothesize → Plan → Approve → Execute → Audit.
  3. Tool layer. The existing Extend REST/SOAP client (read + gated write) and the MCP servers expose Actimize operations to any compliant agent; the same RAG grounding drives the dashboard-render moments.
  4. Write gate. Every mutating call pauses for explicit approval (interrupt() / checkpoint / tool-approval); the analyst confirms by voice or click.
  5. Audit. OpenTelemetry traces (who / what / args / response / approver / timestamp) to an immutable log or SIEM.

docs.title: Build vs. buy
docs.text:
We assemble (mature OSS) We build (the moat) Voice pipeline, orchestration graph, MCP transport, tracing Actimize-domain ontology (alert/work-item taxonomy + per-category workflow templates) Human-gate primitives (until: APPROVED) Proactive batching engine (similarity scoring → one-approval batch) Dashboard/chart rendering Voice → intent → dashboard render protocol — The grounded doc corpus + source_url citation backbone

On Archon

Archon (coleam00) has pivoted to a YAML workflow engine for AI coding agents; its old RAG/MCP build is archived/beta. So it is pattern inspiration, not a foundation — we borrow the declarative YAML workflow + until: APPROVED human-gate idea and implement investigation playbooks on LangGraph / MAF. See architecture-research.md for the full sourced analysis.

→ Continue to Safety & trust.

docs.title: Business case
docs.text:
Illustrative figures

The numbers below are transparent, assumption-based illustrations, not official figures. Validate with Product / Finance before any external use.

docs.title: Mapping to the four evaluation criteria
docs.text: Criterion How ActWise scores Impact New AI-tier SKU/upsell (revenue), lower PS delivery cost and higher services margin, support deflection, faster time-to-value → better win rates and retention. Touches every Actimize customer and product. Feasibility Core planes already built and proven on a live instance. Rides open standards (MCP, Copilot Studio, OpenAPI/Extend REST). Read-only-by-default + gated writes is a concrete, shippable safety model. Innovation First grounded + actionable AI operator for Actimize: doc-cited knowledge fused with safe, discovery-driven live operations. Nobody pairs anti-hallucination grounding with gated writes to the product itself. Scalability Recipe-driven: the same discover→describe→call engine generalizes across every Actimize REST API. Add a product's spec + auth recipe and it works. Customer AI bears token cost → compounding value at near-zero marginal cost.

docs.title: Revenue & cost logic (illustrative)
docs.text:
Lever Type Illustrative formula AI Connector Pack (A) Revenue growth # customers × attach rate × annual add-on price Agentic Operator seats (B) Revenue growth # analyst seats × per-seat add-on Outcome pricing (B) Revenue growth # dispositions/yr × price-per-cleared-item (displaces per-seat — see moat 2) PS Accelerator (D) Cost reduction / margin engagements/yr × days saved × loaded day-rate Support deflection (A/E) Cost reduction / retention deflected tickets × cost-per-ticket Academy (E) Revenue / retention # learners × enablement price + ramp-time savings

Even a low single-digit attach rate across the install base is a material net-new ARR line because it rides existing accounts at near-zero marginal cost. The outcome-pricing lever is the structural upside: because ActWise completes dispositions, NiCE can price on results a text copilot never touches — see the competitive moat.

docs.title: Why it's a 10X idea
docs.text:
• 10× more operators — turns Actimize from "specialists only" into "anyone authorized," and trains the next operators via Academy.
  • 10× faster — days-to-seconds for answers; weeks-to-days for implementation/audit.
  • 10× safer AI — mandatory citations + "I don't know" + read-only-default + gated writes make AI on a regulated system trustworthy.
  • 10× reach — one recipe-driven backend compounds across every Actimize product and every customer, not a one-off.

docs.title: Competitive timing
docs.text:
AML/fraud peers (Verafin, Featurespace ARIC Copilot, SAS, Unit21) ship text copilots — SAR-narrative generation, alert summarization, NL case queries. A voice-driven, multi-agent, proactively-batching, gated-write operator is greenfield as of 2026. That timing is the moat.

→ Full battlecard and the four structural moats: Competitive moat & displacement.

→ See the Demo storyboard.

docs.title: Competitive moat & displacement
docs.text:
The thesis. Every AML/fraud vendor is shipping the same AI feature — a text copilot that summarizes and drafts. ActWise is not a better copilot. It is a different category: the AI operations layer that runs the financial-crime workflow. Copilots assist an analyst; ActWise operates the case — safely, cited, and audited. That difference is structural, and it is what displaces the field.

Why this is credible, not a pitch

The hard part — an AI that acts on a live Actimize instance, cited and gated — is already built and proven: a real workflow step change executed end-to-end on a live ActOne 10.0.0.69 SP15 instance, and grounded answers benchmarked ~8× faster than a cold baseline with identical results. The moats below are what that working layer unlocks — not features we hope to build.

docs.title: 1. The category reframe: operate, don't assist
docs.text:
The entire competitive set is racing to add a copilot — a chat panel bolted onto an existing product that reads case data and writes text. ActWise redefines the frame.

Copilot (the field) ActWise — operations layer What it does Assists a human who does the work Runs the work end-to-end, human approves Relationship to the system Reads case data Discovers, reads, and acts on the live system Output Text (summary, SAR draft) A disposition — a real, audited state change Interface Chat panel Voice or chat, hands-free Trust model LLM bolted on Cited + read-only-default + gated + audited by construction Scope One product's window Recipe-driven across every Actimize product

You cannot win a category by being a slightly better instance of the other guy's category. ActWise wins by being a category competitors structurally can't enter — because their copilots don't act on the system, and re-architecting a shipped product to act safely on a regulated system is an 18-month problem, not a sprint.

docs.title: 2. The battlecard
docs.text:
From our competitive research, the state of the art across the AML vendor landscape is (1) LLM-generated SAR narratives, (2) alert summarization, (3) NL queries over case data. All text. All read-only. No voice.

Competitor What they ship The gap ActWise attacks Verafin (Nasdaq) Generative AI for SAR narratives + case summarization Text only — doesn't act, isn't cited to source, no voice Featurespace ARIC Copilot Analyst-assist copilot, risk scoring Read-only assist; no gated write, no audit-by-construction SAS AML LLM narrative + explainability Explains detection; doesn't operate the case lifecycle Unit21 Alert summarization, rule suggestion Suggests to a human; no safe execution layer Sardine.ai ML fraud scoring A detection model, not an analyst operations layer

Common structural weakness: every one is a read-only text assistant living inside one product's console. None of them can (a) act on the system, (b) prove why with a real source citation, (c) be driven by voice, or (d) generalize across products from a recipe. Those four gaps are our four wedges.

docs.title: 3. Four structural moats (+ graduated autonomy)
docs.text: These are structural, not features — each is anchored in something ActWise already does, and each compounds over time in a way a text copilot cannot follow.

docs.title: Moat 1 — It operates (the acting layer)
docs.text:
The actone ops engine discovers a live instance's REST/SOAP surface, describes it, and calls it — reading work items, config, and diagnostics and executing gated changes. Proven live. A competitor's copilot would need to re-architect a shipped product to act safely on a regulated system to match this.

Built. Real gated step change executed on live ActOne.

docs.title: Moat 2 — Outcome economics (the business-model displacement)
docs.text:
Because ActWise completes dispositions, NiCE can move up the value chain from per-seat licenses to per-disposition / cleared-alert / SLA-outcome pricing. Competitors selling seat licenses for a text copilot cannot price on outcomes — they never touch the outcome. Changing the basis of competition is what real displacement looks like: you stop competing on their axis.

Capability built; pricing model is roadmap (validate with Product/Finance).

docs.title: Moat 3 — The data flywheel (the durable moat)
docs.text:
Every gated approval, analyst correction, and disposition is labeled signal, captured today in the audit trace (who / what / args / approver / timestamp). Fed back into the YAML playbooks, ActWise gets smarter per customer and — federated — across the install base. A read-only text copilot captures none of this loop, so it stays flat while ActWise compounds. This is the moat that widens with use.

Capture built/designed; the learning loop is roadmap.

docs.title: Moat 4 — Auditable-first (the trust wedge that unlocks the regulated market)
docs.text:
AI-risk governance (SR 11-7 / model risk) is the #1 blocker for AI in compliance. Because every ActWise action is cited to a real docs.niceactimize.com URL, read-only by default, gated, and audited by construction, AI-risk flips from a blocker into the sales argument. Competitors bolting an LLM onto a shipped product fight model-risk reviews; ActWise is regulator-ready by architecture.

Built. See Safety & trust.

docs.title: The roadmap wedge — graduated autonomy
docs.text:
The same gated-write engine supports moving the gate from "approve every action" → "approve the policy, auto-execute within it." That unlocks the true throughput 10x (auto-clear low-risk work under policy) — a level a non-acting copilot can never reach, because it doesn't act at all.

Architecture supports it; staged rollout is roadmap. See Roadmap.

docs.title: 4. Ecosystem & network-effect moats
docs.text: The first four moats make ActWise defensible on its own. These three make it defensible because of everyone else on it — the classic moats that compound with scale.

docs.title: Moat 5 — Playbook & connector marketplace (ecosystem lock-in)
docs.text:
Investigation playbooks are YAML ("Dockerfiles for investigations") and connectors are recipe-driven. Open that up: customers, NiCE PS, and SI partners publish and share playbooks and connectors — an AppExchange for financial-crime operations. Two-sided flywheel: more playbooks → more value → more adopters → more authors. A closed text copilot has nothing to list and no ecosystem to lock in.

YAML playbooks + recipe connectors exist today; the marketplace is roadmap.

docs.title: Moat 6 — System of engagement (daily-habit lock-in)
docs.text:
ActWise becomes the surface analysts live in — the voice/chat operations layer above the system of record. Even where a customer runs multiple detection engines, ActWise is where the work happens. Removing it means retraining every operator — distribution and habit lock-in a bolt-on panel never earns.

Front-end surface exists; estate breadth is roadmap.

docs.title: Moat 7 — Federated learning network effect
docs.text:
Anonymized playbook improvements shared across the install base mean each new customer makes everyone's playbooks smarter — a true network effect layered on the per-customer data flywheel (moat 3). The install base itself becomes an asset a new entrant cannot replicate.

Extension of the data flywheel; federation is roadmap.

docs.title: 5. Why competitors can't just copy it
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    F["Ship a text copilot<br/><small>2023–2026: everyone did this</small>"] --> Q{"Re-architect a shipped<br/>product to safely ACT<br/>on a regulated system?"}
    Q -->|"18-month problem"| G["Gated writes + audit-by-construction<br/>+ live spec discovery"]
    G --> H["Then build the flywheel<br/>+ outcome pricing + autonomy"]
    H --> W["Where ActWise already is"]
  • Feature copy ≠ moat. They can add voice to a text panel. They cannot cheaply add a safe acting layer to a product never designed to be operated by an agent.
  • The flywheel is a head start that widens. Every disposition ActWise runs is signal a read-only copilot will never collect.
  • The trust model is architectural, not additive. "Cited + gated + audited by construction" can't be sprinkled on after the fact; it has to be the design.
  • Recipe-driven reach. One discover → describe → call engine generalizes across every Actimize product; competitors rebuild per surface.

docs.title: 6. Why now
docs.text:
• Standards just arrived. MCP, Copilot Studio, and mature Extend/OpenAPI REST make a safe, portable acting layer possible today — the enabling tech is 2025-fresh.
  • The field is still text-only. As of mid-2026 no AML/fraud vendor ships a voice-driven, acting, cited operations layer. The window is open.
  • AI-risk is the gating question in every compliance deal — and ActWise's answer (auditable-first) is exactly what unblocks it. Whoever lands the trusted acting layer first sets the category's rules.

The one-liner

Everyone else built an AI that talks about the case. ActWise built the AI that works the case — safely, cited, and audited. That's not a feature race. It's a different game.

→ See how this converts to value in the Business case.

docs.title: Demo storyboard
docs.text: A sparkathon-style demo spine — one continuous narrative that shows the packaging (A), the hero moment (B), and the breadth (C/D/E), closing on safety and the moat. Target runtime ~4–5 minutes.

docs.title: The spine
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    S1["1 · Problem"] --> S2["2 · Packaging reveal (A)"] --> S3["3 · Hero moment (B)"] --> S4["4 · Breadth (C/D/E)"] --> S5["5 · Why 10X + safety"] --> S6["6 · The moat"]

docs.title: Beat-by-beat
docs.text: # Beat On screen Voiceover hook 1 Problem Actimize consoles, a backlog of alerts "Actimize value is gated behind scarce specialists and separate consoles." 2 Packaging reveal (A) mcp.json → Claude Desktop connects "Install the Connector Pack, point your own AI at it. Cited docs, real data, gated actions — instantly." 3 Hero moment (B) Voice: "Work this alert" → summary → Approve → step change → "5 similar — batch them?" "The agent reads context, cites the policy, recommends the step, and — after one approval — acts. Then it offers to batch the rest." 4 Breadth (C/D/E) Control Tower dashboard renders; ndc→actone-local→gap report; a learning path "One dashboard for the estate. One agent that runs an implementation. One that trains your team." 5 Why 10X + safety Citations, "I don't know", read-only-default, audit line "Grounded, read-only by default, every write gated and audited — 10× more operators, 10× faster, 10× safer." 6 The moat Split screen: a competitor's text copilot vs. ActWise executing + auditing a disposition "Everyone else built an AI that talks about the case. We built the one that works it — safely, cited, audited. That's not a feature race; it's a different category."

docs.title: Signature lines to capture on camera
docs.text:
• "Show me all open alerts" → dashboard renders instantly.
  • "Work this alert — do what needs to be done" → context → recommend → draft → approve → execute.
  • "There are 5 more of the same category — want me to handle them too?" → batch under one approval.
  • "Generate this week's SAR-pipeline report" → automated, cited report.
  • "They summarize the case. We close it." → the category line, on camera.

docs.title: Reusable assets in the repo
docs.text:
• docs/sparkathon/VIDEO-SCRIPT.md — shot-by-shot format to mirror.
  • docs/sparkathon/assets/actwise-architecture-diagrams.drawio — architecture visuals.
  • components/wiki/content/assets/actwise-demo-overview.mp4 — existing demo footage of the live agent (cited docs + gated ops) as a head start.

Production route

Follow the sparkathon video-exception path: record an .mp4 clearly showing the prototype actually working (not a mockup), then attach it to the pitch deck.

docs.title: Companion deck
docs.text: The pitch deck (sparkathon-style) is planned as assets/ActWise-Platform-10X.pptx — see the Roadmap for the build sequence.

docs.title: The platform
docs.text: ActWise is one guarded backend exposed through interchangeable front ends and sold as five tiers. The tiers are not separate products — they are compounding scope on the same connectors, the same grounding, and the same safety model.

docs.title: One backend, two front ends
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    subgraph FE["Front ends (interchangeable)"]
        BYO["Bring-your-own AI<br/><small>Claude · Copilot · Cursor · ChatGPT</small>"]
        CS["NiCE-branded app<br/><small>Copilot Studio · Teams/M365 · web portal</small>"]
    end
    GW["Entitlement gateway<br/><small>SSO · connect & activate products · audit</small>"]
    subgraph BE["Guarded backend — the ActWise connectors"]
        K["Knowledge<br/><small>docenter</small>"]
        AC["Action<br/><small>actone ops</small>"]
        DT["Data<br/><small>actone-data</small>"]
        UT["Utilities<br/><small>actone-utils</small>"]
        SP["Supply/Provision<br/><small>ndc · installer</small>"]
    end
    D[("Docs portal")]
    A["Live ActOne<br/><small>Extend REST/SOAP</small>"]
    DB[("ActOne DB<br/><small>v_acm_* views</small>")]

    BYO & CS --> GW --> K & AC & DT & UT & SP
    K --> D
    AC --> A
    DT --> DB
    UT --> A
    SP --> A

The economics of two front ends

The bring-your-own-AI path means the customer's AI does the reasoning — NiCE carries near-zero LLM token cost and no GPU footprint while still owning the guarded backend, the data access, and the audit trail. The Copilot Studio path is the branded, managed end state. One backend, two front ends — fast to demo, cheap to scale.

docs.title: The five tiers
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A["A · Connector Suite<br/><small>packaging / distribution</small>"]
    B["B · Agentic Workflow<br/><small>hero product</small>"]
    C["C · Control Tower<br/><small>visibility</small>"]
    D["D · Accelerator<br/><small>services margin</small>"]
    E["E · Academy<br/><small>enablement</small>"]
    A --> B --> C
    B --> D
    B --> E
Tier Sells as Primary metric moved A · Connector Suite AI-tier add-on / marketplace listing Attach rate × add-on ARR B · Agentic Workflow ⭐ Seat-based operator add-on Analyst throughput; time-to-disposition C · Control Tower Estate/MSSP tier Visibility; report-generation cost D · Accelerator Services enablement Engineer-days per engagement E · Academy Enablement/training line Ramp time; certification throughput

docs.title: How the tiers reuse the same assets
docs.text:
Connector (repo) A B C D E docenter (Knowledge) ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅ actone ops (Action) ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅ — actone-data (Data) ✅ ✅ ✅ — — actone-utils (Utilities) ✅ ✅ — ✅ — ndc (Supply) ✅ — — ✅ — installer / actone-local ✅ — — ✅ ✅ (sandbox) portal (Surface) — ✅ ✅ — ✅

Every tier is built from connectors that already exist — the platform is a packaging and orchestration story, not new plumbing.

→ Explore the hero: Agentic Workflow (B).

docs.title: The opportunity
docs.text: Actimize delivers best-in-class financial-crime and compliance capability — but its value is gated behind expertise. That gap is the opportunity ActWise attacks.

docs.title: Where the value gets stuck
docs.text:
• :material-account-hard-hat: Operating is specialist-only

    Configuring work-item types, plugins, workflows and policies, and investigating alerts/cases requires certified specialists and tribal knowledge of the Extend REST APIs.

  • :material-clock-alert: Implementations burn weeks

    Professional-services engineers hand-build collections, hunt through version-segmented docs for one config parameter, and script one-off API calls.

  • :material-lifebuoy: Support re-answers the same questions

    "How do I configure X?" / "Why did Y break?" — each a ticket, each a context-switch, each re-solved from scratch.

  • :material-robot-off: General LLMs can't safely help

    Off-the-shelf models hallucinate Actimize features and have no grounded, safe path to act on a live, regulated system.

docs.title: The result
docs.text:
Slow time-to-value, high services/support cost, and an AI story that stops at "chatbot."

The bottleneck is the operating layer — not the capability

The financial-crime capability already exists and is excellent. What's missing is a grounded, safe, AI-operable layer that lets more people extract that value, faster, without becoming certified experts first.

docs.title: Who feels it
docs.text: Audience Pain ActWise tier that helps FMC analysts / investigators Alert/case backlog, repetitive triage, console-hopping B · Agentic Workflow Ops / compliance leaders No single view of the estate; manual reporting C · Control Tower Professional Services / SIs Weeks per implementation/upgrade; scarce specialists D · Accelerator Support / CS Repeat how-to tickets; reactive firefighting A · Connector Suite + E · Academy New hires / customer L&D Slow ramp on complex products; stale training E · Academy

docs.title: Why now
docs.text:
• MCP is a real standard. Any client's AI (Claude, Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT) can be pointed at Actimize connectors today — no bespoke integration.
  • The connectors already exist. Knowledge, Action, Data, Utilities, Supply and Provision are built and proven in the ActWise repo.
  • Peers ship text copilots only. AML/fraud competitors offer narrative generation and alert summarization — a voice-driven, gated-write, proactively-batching operator is greenfield as of 2026.

→ Continue to The platform.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
A phased build from the proven core to the full tiered platform. Each phase ships something demoable; nothing depends on unproven plumbing.

flowchart LR
    P0["Phase 0<br/><small>Proven core</small>"] --> P1["Phase 1<br/><small>Package (A)</small>"] --> P2["Phase 2<br/><small>Hero (B)</small>"] --> P3["Phase 3<br/><small>Breadth (C/D/E)</small>"] --> P4["Phase 4<br/><small>Branded platform</small>"] --> P5["Phase 5<br/><small>Graduated autonomy</small>"]

docs.title: Phase 0 — Proven core (done)
docs.text:
• Knowledge plane (docenter) + Action plane (ops, gated writes) built and proven live.
  • Data / utilities / supply / provision connectors shipped.
  • Copilot Studio agents + MCP servers running.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Package (Tier A)
docs.text:
• Bundle all MCPs/connectors as the "Actimize AI Connector Pack" with an entitlement gateway (SSO, per-user product activation, audit).
  • One-click / mcp.json install for Claude / Copilot / Cursor.
  • Milestone: a client installs the pack and gets cited docs + data + gated ops.

docs.title: Phase 2 — Hero (Tier B)
docs.text:
• Voice/chat layer + orchestration graph (LangGraph / MAF).
  • Investigation playbooks as YAML; human-gate + audit.
  • Proactive batching engine (similarity scoring → one-approval batch).
  • Milestone: the "work this alert → approve → batch 5 more" demo, end-to-end.

docs.title: Phase 3 — Breadth (Tiers C/D/E)
docs.text:
• Control Tower — estate dashboard + voice→dashboard render + scheduled cited reports.
  • Accelerator — the ndc → actone-local → ops audit → cited gap report chain.
  • Academy — role-based paths + quizzes + sandbox on local ActOne.
  • Milestone: one dashboard, one implementation run, one learning path — all demoable.

docs.title: Phase 4 — Branded platform
docs.text:
• Copilot Studio / Teams-M365 branded front end over the same backend.
  • Marketplace listing; per-tier packaging & pricing finalized with Product/Finance.
  • Generalize the discover→describe→call engine to more Actimize REST APIs (RCM, IFM, SAM, WLM…) via spec + auth recipes.

docs.title: Phase 5 — Graduated autonomy
docs.text:
The gated-write engine stays the same — only where the human approves moves, one safe rung at a time. This is the path to the true throughput 10x (auto-clearing routine work under policy) and it is a level a non-acting text copilot can never reach.

flowchart LR
    L0["Approve<br/>every action"] --> L1["Approve<br/>per batch"] --> L2["Approve the policy,<br/>auto-execute within it"] --> L3["Auto-clear low-risk<br/>under policy + sampling"]
  • Each rung is opt-in per alert category, bounded by policy, and fully audited; risk can never exceed what the customer explicitly enables.
  • Autonomy is earned from the data flywheel — a playbook graduates only after its gated track record clears an accuracy bar.
  • Humans stay in the loop by exception (sampling + escalation), not by rote.

  • Milestone: one alert category runs at "approve the policy" with a clean audit and an accuracy report — the proof that autonomy is safe because it's cited and audited.

  • [x] Concept options (concepts-and-options.md)

  • [x] Platform wiki (this site)
  • [ ] Sparkathon-style pitch deck (assets/ActWise-Platform-10X.pptx)
  • [ ] Demo script / storyboard finalized against live footage
  • [ ] Validate illustrative business figures with Product/Finance

docs.title: Risks & mitigations
docs.text: Risk Mitigation Tenant SSO / app-registration blocks the branded web front end Lead with the bring-your-own-AI path (A); branded front end is Phase 4. Regulated-write trust concerns Read-only default + gated writes + full audit (see Safety). "Already on the roadmap" disqualifier Position as a net-new AI capability/SKU, not a planned item. LLM cost at scale Customer AI bears token cost via the MCP path — near-zero marginal cost to NiCE. Autonomy over-reach Autonomy is opt-in per category, policy-bounded, and gated by a flywheel accuracy bar; humans stay in by exception (Phase 5).

docs.title: Safety & trust
docs.text: AI on a regulated financial-crime system is only deployable if it is trustworthy by construction. ActWise's safety model is the unlock that makes every other 10X claim shippable.

docs.title: Four guarantees
docs.text:
• :material-format-quote-close: Grounded, or silent

    Knowledge answers are quoted from official docs with a real source_url. If retrieval finds nothing, the agent says "I don't know" rather than inventing a feature.

  • :material-lock-open-check: Read-only by default

    Data is strictly read-only. Ops and utilities read freely so the AI can investigate — but cannot mutate anything without passing the gate.

  • :material-account-check: Every write is gated

    Each state-changing action pauses for explicit human approval (voice or click), with a preview of exactly what will change before it happens.

  • :material-file-document-check: Everything is audited

    Immutable traces — who, what, args, response, approver, timestamp — to a log or SIEM. The AI investigates freely; it only acts under guardrails.

docs.title: Where each guarantee lives
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    subgraph read["Read (always on)"]
        DOC["docs · cited"]
        DATA["data · read-only SQL"]
        OPS["ops · GET / describe"]
    end
    GATE{"Human approval gate"}
    subgraph write["Write (gated)"]
        STEP["step change"]
        UTIL["utility run"]
    end
    AUD["Immutable audit log / SIEM"]
    read --> GATE --> write --> AUD

docs.title: Trust principles (inherited from the toolkit)
docs.text:
• No fabrication. Docs answers are quoted with citations; data answers come only from real query results; ops reports return real API responses, including errors.
  • Your credentials, your data. The platform ships no NICE content — it uses the customer's own authenticated portal / ActOne / DB sessions.
  • Deterministic gates. Writes require an explicit confirmation or --execute/--yes equivalent; nothing mutates implicitly.

Why this beats a general LLM

A general-purpose model hallucinates Actimize features and has no safe path to act. ActWise pairs anti-hallucination grounding with gated write access to the product itself — nobody else is doing both.

→ Continue to the Business case.

docs.title: Tier E — ActWise Academy
docs.text:
Turn the doc corpus into living training. The same DOCenter knowledge that grounds the platform becomes role-based learning paths, guided onboarding, auto-generated quizzes, certification prep, and a practice sandbox against a real ActOne.

Role in the platform: enablement — the platform doesn't just operate Actimize, it teaches people to.

docs.title: What it generates
docs.text:
• :material-map-marker-path: Role-based paths — analyst, investigator, admin, implementer, support — auto-assembled from the relevant product/version docs.
  • :material-gesture-tap-button: Guided onboarding — interactive, cited walkthroughs with the real source page one click away.
  • :material-help-box: Quizzes & cert prep — generated from the corpus, kept current as docs change (no stale slide decks).
  • :material-flask: Practice sandbox — rehearse workflows against a local ActOne instance with zero production risk.

docs.title: In-context coaching
docs.text:
While operating, the co-pilot can explain why — cited — turning every action into a teachable moment. Operating and learning share one grounded brain.

flowchart LR
    C[("Doc corpus<br/><small>docenter</small>")]
    L["Learning paths<br/><small>role-based</small>"]
    Q["Quizzes + cert prep"]
    SB["Sandbox<br/><small>actone-local</small>"]
    CO["In-context coaching<br/><small>'explain why', cited</small>"]
    C --> L & Q & CO
    SB --> CO

docs.title: How a client automates their workflow
docs.text: New hires ramp against real, current docs + a safe sandbox instead of stale decks; certification prep is generated, not manually maintained; and the operating co-pilot doubles as an always-on coach.

docs.title: Demo storyline (≈2 min)
docs.text:
1. Generate an "ActOne Analyst" learning path → cited pages + a quiz.
  2. Launch a sandbox exercise on local ActOne.
  3. Ask "explain why this step" → cited coaching in context.

docs.title: Why it's 10X
docs.text:
• Trains the next generation of operators, compounding adoption (confident users → more usage → retention).
  • A natural upsell / enablement line that reuses an asset we already have — the doc corpus and the wiki.

→ See the whole picture: Architecture.

docs.title: Tier D — Implementation & Upgrade Accelerator
docs.text:
Compress professional-services delivery from weeks to days. Point the agent at a new or target instance → auto-detect version, review configuration, generate API automation, and produce a cited upgrade/gap report — pulling the right installer and standing it up to validate.

Role in the platform: services margin — attacks the cost of every implementation and upgrade.

docs.title: The delivery chain — end to end, agent-driven
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    S["Supply<br/><small>ndc: find + download package</small>"]
    P["Provision<br/><small>actone-local: stand up on Docker</small>"]
    A["Audit<br/><small>actone ops: review config</small>"]
    G["Ground<br/><small>docenter: cite upgrade notes</small>"]
    R["Report<br/><small>cited gap / upgrade plan</small>"]
    S --> P --> A --> G --> R
Step Connector What it does Supply ndc Search + download the correct installer / service pack / patch. Provision actone-local / actinstaller Stand up or upgrade ActOne (Docker + PostgreSQL) to validate. Audit actone ops Review work-item types, plugins, licenses, tenants; generate Postman/API automation. Ground docenter Cite the version's upgrade notes and config contracts. Report orchestration "You're on X, target Y — here are the gaps," fully cited.

docs.title: How a client automates their workflow
docs.text: The PS engineer stops hand-building collections and hunting through version-segmented docs. The agent runs the whole chain and hands back a cited gap report plus ready-to-run API automation — in minutes, not engineer-days.

docs.title: Demo storyline (≈3 min)
docs.text:
1. ndc finds and downloads the target service pack.
  2. actone-local stands the instance up on Docker.
  3. actone ops audits the config and generates a Postman collection.
  4. A cited upgrade/gap report is produced: current → target, with the exact doc pages backing each finding.

docs.title: Why it's 10X
docs.text:
• Weeks → days per implementation/upgrade.
  • Frees scarce certified specialists for higher-value work.
  • Savings flow straight to services margin — engagements/yr × days saved × loaded day-rate.

→ Next: Academy (E).

docs.title: Tier B — ActOne Agentic Investigation Workflow ⭐
docs.text:
The hero product. A YAML-defined, human-in-the-loop investigation & automation engine that runs an alert or case from arrival to disposition — driven by voice or chat, grounded in cited docs, and safe by construction.

Role in the platform: the differentiated demo moment. The core action loop is already proven live against a real ActOne instance.

docs.title: The workflow
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    T["Triage<br/><small>classify · prioritise</small>"]
    E["Enrich<br/><small>read context: ops + data</small>"]
    H["Hypothesize<br/><small>cited reasoning</small>"]
    P["Action-Plan<br/><small>next Dynamic Workflow step</small>"]
    AP{"Approve<br/><small>human, one tap</small>"}
    X["Execute<br/><small>gated write</small>"]
    AU["Audit<br/><small>immutable trace</small>"]
    T --> E --> H --> P --> AP
    AP -->|approved| X --> AU
    AP -->|changes| P
    AU -.->|"5 similar alerts?"| T

Each stage maps to real connectors:

Stage Powered by Notes Triage / Enrich actone-data + actone ops Read work item, custom attributes, valid next steps. Hypothesize docenter Explains why with citations — never invents a rule. Action-Plan ActOne Dynamic Workflow model Recommends the correct next step / business action. Approve human-in-the-loop gate Voice or one-tap; nothing mutates before this. Execute actone ops (gated write) Note + step change, exactly as proven live. Audit audit layer who / what / args / approver / timestamp.

docs.title: Proactive batching — the signature moment
docs.text:
After resolving one alert, the agent offers:

One approval, many dispositions

"There are 5 more alerts of the same category — want me to handle them the same way?" → similarity scoring → one approval → batch execution → full audit for each.

This is the throughput multiplier: analysts stop doing the same triage five times.

docs.title: Playbooks as YAML ("Dockerfiles for investigations")
docs.text:
Investigation logic is declarative and editable per alert category — inspired by the until: APPROVED human-gate pattern, implemented on a durable, auditable graph:

playbook: high-risk-wire
steps:
  - id: enrich      # read context via ops + data
  - id: hypothesize # cite policy from docenter
  - id: plan        # recommend next Dynamic Workflow step
  - id: approve
    interactive: true
    until: APPROVED  # pauses for human sign-off
  - id: execute     # gated step change
  - id: batch       # offer similar alerts under one approval

docs.title: Demo storyline (≈3 min)
docs.text:
1. Voice: "Show me open alerts." → dashboard renders.
  2. "Work this one — do what needs doing." → agent summarizes, cites the policy, recommends QA Consult, drafts the note.
  3. Approve → executes the step change.
  4. "5 similar alerts — batch them?" → Yes → one approval, five dispositions, full audit.

docs.title: Why it's 10X
docs.text:
• Analyst throughput multiplier with regulator-grade gated writes + audit.
  • Peers (Verafin, Featurespace, SAS, Unit21) ship text copilots — a voice-driven, proactively-batching, gated-write operator is greenfield. That's the moat.
  • Targets the largest user population (analysts) and the core Actimize value loop.

Already demonstrable

In prior sessions the prototype connected to a live ActOne, read an alert's step + custom attributes + valid next steps, and executed a note + QA-Consult step change end-to-end — the exact loop behind the voice moments above.

→ Next: Control Tower (C).

docs.title: Tier A — ActWise Connector Suite
docs.text:
The "Actimize AI Pack" — bring your own AI. Package every ActWise MCP server and connector into one versioned, installable bundle. A client points their own AI (Claude Desktop, Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT desktop, any MCP client) at it and instantly has a grounded, actioning Actimize co-pilot — no NiCE-hosted LLM required.

Role in the platform: packaging / distribution. This is how the whole platform ships and scales at near-zero marginal cost.

docs.title: What's in the pack
docs.text:
Capability Connector Default access 🔎 Cited documentation answers docenter-mcp / actimize-docs-mcp read 🛰️ Live ActOne operations actone-mcp (Extend REST + SOAP) read; writes gated 📊 Plain-English data queries actone-data-mcp (NL→SQL over v_acm_*) read-only 🧰 Server-side utilities actone-utils-mcp (33 Java tools) gated ⬇️ Install-media supply ndc read

All behind an entitlement gateway: SSO, per-user product activation, and a full audit trail.

docs.title: How a client automates their workflow
docs.text:
One mcp.json (or a one-click installer) wires up all five capabilities:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "actwise-docs": { "command": "docenter-mcp" },
    "actwise-ops":  { "command": "actone-mcp" },
    "actwise-data": { "command": "actone-data-mcp" },
    "actwise-utils":{ "command": "actone-utils-mcp" }
  }
}

From that moment the client's own AI can search docs, query alert data, and (with approval) act on ActOne — inside the tool they already use every day.

docs.title: Demo storyline (≈3 min)
docs.text:
1. Install the pack and point Claude Desktop at it.
  2. "How do I configure a Conditional Step Change plugin in ActOne 10.1?" → a cited docs.niceactimize.com answer.
  3. "How many open high-risk alerts do we have?" → NL→SQL over v_acm_* returns real numbers.
  4. "Advance alert QAS_…058 to QA Consult." → a gated step change: preview → approve → execute → audit line.

docs.title: Why it's 10X
docs.text:
• Turns every customer's existing AI subscription into an Actimize operator.
  • NiCE carries near-zero token/GPU cost — distribution is an MCP registry listing, not a GPU fleet.
  • This is the literal "package all the tools/MCPs/connectors for clients" play.

docs.title: Packaging & go-to-market
docs.text:
• SKU: "ActWise AI Connector Pack" — an AI-tier add-on attached to the Actimize install base.
  • Delivery: versioned bundle + entitlement key; listed in an MCP/agent marketplace.
  • Pricing lever: attach rate × per-account add-on ARR (see Business case).

→ Next: the hero product, Agentic Workflow (B).

docs.title: Tier C — ActWise Control Tower
docs.text:
One pane of glass over the whole Actimize estate. A cross-product health & operations dashboard with a natural-language "Ask" bar that renders dashboards on demand and generates cited, audit-ready reports on a schedule.

Role in the platform: visibility — and the natural MSSP / multi-tenant expansion.

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
Connect each Actimize product once (ActOne, AIS, AML/SAM, CDD, IFM, WL/Sanctions, RCM); the Control Tower surfaces a unified view:

  • :material-heart-pulse: Health — instance versions, diagnostics, license/usage.
  • :material-inbox-multiple: Volumes — alert/case counts, queue backlogs.
  • :material-timer-alert: Risk — SLA / deadline-at-risk items across products.
  • :material-chart-box: Trends — throughput, aging, disposition mix over time.

docs.title: Ask → render
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    Q["'Show me SLA-risk cases by queue'"]
    N["NL → SQL<br/><small>actone-data</small>"]
    R["Dashboard renders<br/><small>live chart, no console</small>"]
    S["'Email me this weekly'"]
    J["Scheduled cited report"]
    Q --> N --> R --> S --> J

The same NL→SQL engine that answers a data question renders a chart for it, and can schedule it as a recurring, cited report — no dashboards to hand-build.

docs.title: How a client automates their workflow
docs.text:
• Standing dashboards per product, refreshed live.
  • On-demand drill-downs by voice into any card.
  • Scheduled reports (SAR-pipeline, backlog, health) generated automatically with citations — audit-ready, no analyst assembling slides.

docs.title: Built on
docs.text: portal (per-user web surface + tunnels) · actone-data (v_acm_*) · actone ops diagnostics · docenter grounding · a scheduled-report job.

docs.title: Demo storyline (≈2 min)
docs.text:
1. Land on the estate dashboard — a health card per connected product.
  2. Type "open alerts by risk" → a live chart renders.
  3. "Email me this weekly" → a scheduled, cited report is created.

docs.title: Why it's 10X
docs.text:
• Exec-level visibility and operate-by-voice across products that today live in separate consoles.
  • A clean MSSP / managed-service story: one tower over many tenants.

→ Next: Implementation Accelerator (D).
